Title: A novel phosphoinositide kinase Fab1 regulates biosynthesis of pathogenic aflatoxin in Aspergillus flavus

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: vesicle-vacuole fraction; cytosolic fraction; secreted fraction; infected peanut seeds; infected maize seeds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: potent toxin and carcinogen; contributes to seed infection/pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fab1 positively regulates aflatoxin biosynthesis, storage/export, and pathogenicity; deletion nearly abolishes AFB1 and greatly reduces AFB2
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: phosphatidylinositol phosphate kinase with FYVE zinc finger, Cpn60_TCP1, and PIPKc domains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: significantly upregulated under three distinct growth conditions; transcript detected by RNA-seq, qRT-PCR, northern blot
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced under different growth conditions including stress-related conditions examined
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YES aflatoxin-inducing medium; PDA; YPD; low temperature (29°C); pH 5 stress; H2O2 oxidative stress; 1 M NaCl hyperosmotic stress; seed infection assays on peanut and maize
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion (Δfab1); complementation (Δfab1::fab1); GFP localization; TLC; HPLC-MS/MS/Xevo TQ MS metabolite quantification; vesicle-vacuole fractionation; neutral red staining; pH and ion measurements; TMT proteomics; PRM; RT-PCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fab1 encodes a vacuolar membrane-associated phosphoinositide kinase conserved in fungi. 2. Deletion of fab1 reduced fungal growth, abolished conidiophore and sclerotial formation, and strongly decreased aflatoxin production. 3. In infected peanut and maize seeds, Δfab1 showed reduced colonization, sporulation, and essentially no detectable aflatoxin. 4. fab1 deletion caused enlarged vacuoles, increased vacuolar/cytosolic acidification, and disturbed Ca2+, Na+, and K+ homeostasis. 5. Quantitative proteomics indicated broad disruption of metabolic, transport, stress-response, and secondary-metabolism proteins, supporting an indirect homeostasis-based mechanism for aflatoxin regulation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: SRX237459; SRX237295; SRX1330586; SRX396791. Quantitative proteomics (TMT), PRM, raw MS data: iProX IPX0001753000
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Fab1 is not an aflatoxin biosynthetic enzyme itself; it regulates aflatoxin accumulation indirectly through vacuolar/cellular homeostasis, vesicle-vacuole function, acidification, ion balance, and likely transport/storage/export processes. The paper hypothesizes altered PtdIns(3,5)P2 as the mechanistic basis, but direct lipid measurement was not reported.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: fab1 deletion caused severe developmental defects, reduced growth, abolished conidiation/sclerotia, altered vacuole morphology, disrupted pH and ion homeostasis, and reduced pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native fab1 upstream promoter region used for complementation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal/mycotoxin-control target for reducing aflatoxin contamination by disrupting vacuole homeostasis and pathogenicity in A. flavus; however, this is more relevant to fungal control than crop breeding, and broad cellular effects suggest pleiotropic consequences.
